symptom: High latency with packet loss and queue depth spike on TOR switch
Elevated latency and packet loss on leaf switch, queue depth above 80%
mitigation: Rebalance ECMP; verify next-hop weights and drain congested paths

symptom: High latency and increasing packet loss on spine switch
mitigation: Check optical power and errors; clean or reseat optics; replace if needed

symptom: Queue depth spike and latency increase on TOR with no packet loss
root_cause: Micro-burst from single flow or aggregate
mitigation: Identify large flows; apply QoS or rate limit; consider buffer tuning

symptom: Packet loss and high queue depth, latency within normal range
root_cause: Dropped tail packets under burst; buffer too small
mitigation: Increase egress buffer or apply backpressure; verify PFC/ECN if applicable

symptom: Sustained high latency on multiple TORs in same rack
mitigation: Check spine utilization and errors; drain or re-route traffic; verify fabric health

symptom: Intermittent packet loss with queue depth fluctuating 70–90%
root_cause: Control-plane or CPU saturation affecting forwarding
mitigation: Check CPU and control-plane load; reduce logging or management traffic; restart if needed

symptom: High latency detected on TOR switch. Packet loss increased. Queue depth reached 85%. Traffic congestion suspected.
mitigation: Drain traffic, reset switch, verify ECMP routes
